column.name: record_type
column.description: Indicates the type of row in the table, such as ORDER for sales transaction lines or VISIT for outlet calls without product-level sales. Analysts should use this to separate transactional sales records from coverage or visit activity records.

column.name: division
column.description: Business division for the record, typically NE in this table. Use it to filter or aggregate performance for the relevant commercial division.

column.name: segment
column.description: Customer or channel segment served by the division, such as Parlor. This helps analysts distinguish parlor-focused activity from other channel segments if combined with other data.

column.name: order_date
column.description: Date associated with the order or visit activity. For ORDER records it represents the sales order date, while for VISIT records it represents the visit date.

column.name: business_month
column.description: Year-month period used for monthly reporting and target tracking, formatted like YYYY-MM. Analysts should use this for month-level aggregation instead of deriving month from dates when official reporting periods matter.

column.name: report_date
column.description: Reporting date on which the activity or metric is recognized. It may match the order or visit date and is useful for daily trend reporting.

column.name: uuid
column.description: Composite unique identifier tying together employee position, employee name, and contact or assignment information. Analysts can use it as a field-force level key, but should validate uniqueness before using it as a primary identifier.

column.name: position_code
column.description: Sales position or territory code assigned to the employee, such as NEP_GUJ_FF6. It is useful for territory-level performance, routing, and hierarchy mapping.

column.name: emp_id
column.description: Unique employee identifier for the field employee responsible for the visit or order. Use this for joining with employee master data or tracking individual productivity.

column.name: emp_name
column.description: Name of the field employee associated with the transaction or visit. This is a descriptive field for reporting and should not be used as a unique key.

column.name: designation
column.description: Role or job title of the employee, such as TSO or Sr. TSI. Analysts can use it to compare performance by role level.

column.name: tl_name
column.description: Name of the team leader or immediate supervisor mapped to the employee. Null values indicate missing or unavailable supervisor mapping.

column.name: tl_designation
column.description: Designation of the team leader, such as ASM. Use it to understand the supervisory level above the field employee.

column.name: rsm_name
column.description: Name of the regional or national sales manager mapped to the employee hierarchy. It supports rollups of sales and activity performance by senior manager.

column.name: rsm_designation
column.description: Designation of the RSM-level manager, such as NSM. This provides context for the hierarchy level represented by rsm_name.

column.name: asm_name
column.description: Name of the Area Sales Manager responsible for the employee or territory. Use it for ASM-level performance reporting and accountability.

column.name: asm_designation
column.description: Designation of the area manager, typically ASM. This helps interpret the management hierarchy for the employee assignment.

column.name: emp_zone
column.description: Sales zone of the employee, such as West. Analysts can use it for regional aggregation of employee activity and sales.

column.name: emp_state
column.description: State or regional market assigned to the employee, such as Gujarat or Mah-Mum. This may reflect business geography rather than standard administrative state names.

column.name: emp_city
column.description: Employee headquarters city or assigned city, such as Vadodara. Null values indicate that the city is not available in the employee mapping.

column.name: hq_state
column.description: Headquarter state or sub-region for the employee, such as west-2 or Mah-Mum. This field may represent internal HQ grouping rather than a formal state name.

column.name: emp_type
column.description: Employee type or classification, if available. In the sample it is null, so analysts should treat it as optional metadata.

column.name: state_group
column.description: Internal grouping of states or markets, if available. Null values indicate that this grouping was not populated for the row.

column.name: is_active
column.description: Flag indicating whether the employee is active in the master data. Null values mean the active status is unavailable and should not be interpreted as inactive.

column.name: is_field_employee
column.description: Boolean-style flag indicating whether the employee is part of the field sales force. True values identify employees whose visits and outlet coverage should be counted in field productivity metrics.

column.name: deactive_date
column.description: Date on which the employee was deactivated, if applicable. Null values usually indicate no deactivation date is recorded or the employee is still active.

column.name: client_id
column.description: Unique identifier of the outlet, salon, parlor, or client involved in the visit or order. Use this as the primary key for outlet-level aggregation where available.

column.name: client_name
column.description: Name of the client or outlet, such as a beauty studio or salon. This is descriptive and may not be unique across geographies.

column.name: client_type
column.description: Type of client or outlet, typically Parlor in this table. Analysts can use it to filter the relevant channel or validate segment alignment.

column.name: contact_type
column.description: Classification of the outlet contact or salon type, such as Ladies or Mens. It helps segment parlors by customer service orientation or target audience.

column.name: client_zone
column.description: Sales zone of the client or outlet, such as West. It may be null for visit-only rows or where outlet geography is incomplete.

column.name: client_state
column.description: State or market where the client outlet is located, such as Gujarat. Use this for outlet-location analysis, distinct from employee-assigned geography.

column.name: client_city
column.description: City where the client outlet is located, such as Vadodara. It supports city-level sales, coverage, and outlet universe reporting.

column.name: is_in_universe
column.description: Flag indicating whether the client is part of the defined outlet universe for coverage and productivity tracking. True outlets are eligible for universe-based KPIs; null means universe status is unavailable.

column.name: is_new_outlet
column.description: Flag indicating whether the client is considered a newly added outlet in the reporting period or program. Analysts should use it for new outlet acquisition and new outlet sales metrics.

column.name: product_id
column.description: Unique product master identifier for the item sold. It is populated on ORDER records and null on visit records without product sales.

column.name: variant_id
column.description: Unique identifier for the specific product variant or SKU variant sold. Use it for variant-level analysis when product_id alone is not granular enough.

column.name: product_code
column.description: Business product code or SKU code associated with the sold product. It may appear as a numeric string with decimals and should generally be treated as text to preserve formatting.

column.name: vsku_code
column.description: Variant SKU code representing the sellable SKU variant. Analysts can use it for SKU-level sales reporting and joins to SKU master data.

column.name: product_name
column.description: Commercial product name sold in the order line, such as Facial Kit or Hair Spa products. Null values occur on non-order visit records.

column.name: variant_name
column.description: Name of the specific SKU variant sold, often including pack size or formulation. This is useful for detailed product mix analysis.

column.name: category
column.description: High-level product category, such as Facial Kit, HOME CARE, DETAN, or Spa. Use it for category-level sales and portfolio performance reporting.

column.name: subcategory
column.description: More detailed product grouping within category, such as Mainline Kits, Serum, Cream, or Luxury Edit. It helps analyze mix at a level below category.

column.name: product_category
column.description: Business-defined product category or franchise label, often combining product family and pack description. It may be more commercially meaningful than the generic category field.

column.name: mrp
column.description: Maximum retail price of the product or variant. Analysts can use it as price reference metadata, not necessarily the actual transaction price.

column.name: is_npd
column.description: Flag indicating whether the product is a new product development or newly launched item. Use it to isolate NPD performance from existing portfolio sales.

column.name: distributer_name
column.description: Name of the distributor fulfilling or associated with the order, such as R.S Marketing-Parlor. The column name appears misspelled but represents distributor name.

column.name: distributer_type
column.description: Type of distributor or channel partner, such as Distributor. Use it to distinguish distributor, super-stockist, or other source structures where populated.

column.name: source_name
column.description: Name of the source entity for the order or supply chain flow, if captured. Null values indicate no source entity was recorded for the row.

column.name: source_type
column.description: Type of source entity, if available. This can help differentiate sales sources or channel origins, but is often null in the sample.

column.name: ss_id
column.description: Identifier for the super stockist or secondary sales source, where applicable. Null values indicate that no super stockist mapping is present for the record.

column.name: beat_name
column.description: Sales beat or route name associated with the client visit or order. Use it to analyze outlet coverage and sales performance by route plan.

column.name: quantity
column.description: Number of units or packs sold for the product line. It is populated for ORDER records and should be summed for volume analysis.

column.name: gross_value
column.description: Gross sales value for the order line before applicable deductions used for net sales calculations. Analysts should aggregate it for top-line order value reporting.

column.name: p3_val
column.description: Sales value classified under the P3 metric or portfolio bucket. It is typically populated when the sold product qualifies for P3 reporting.

column.name: p3_nsv
column.description: Net sales value for the P3 bucket after adjustments such as tax or deductions. This is commonly used for achievement against P3 NSV targets.

column.name: p2_val
column.description: Sales value classified under the P2 metric or portfolio bucket. Zero values indicate the transaction did not contribute to P2 for that line.

column.name: p2_nsv
column.description: Net sales value for the P2 bucket after adjustments. Use it for P2 performance and target achievement analysis.

column.name: tc
column.description: Total call indicator/count for an outlet visit on the row. VISIT rows often carry tc=1, while product order lines may carry tc=0 to avoid double counting calls.

column.name: pc
column.description: Productive call indicator/count showing whether the outlet call resulted in a productive sale. Analysts should sum it carefully at outlet-call grain to avoid duplicate counting across multiple order lines.

column.name: tlsd
column.description: Total lines sold indicator/count for the row or transaction context. It is used to measure breadth of selling, but analysts should confirm grain before summing across order lines.

column.name: ulsd
column.description: Unique lines sold indicator/count for the row or transaction context. It supports product line penetration analysis and may require deduplication at order or outlet level.

column.name: eco
column.description: Effective coverage outlet indicator/count, typically 1 when an outlet contributes to effective coverage or productive activity. Use it for outlet coverage KPIs, taking care to avoid duplication on multi-line orders.

column.name: mtd_tc
column.description: Month-to-date total calls for the employee, territory, or reporting context. It is populated on summary or KPI rows and null on many detail transaction rows.

column.name: mtd_pc
column.description: Month-to-date productive calls for the relevant employee or territory context. Use it to track cumulative productivity within the business month.

column.name: mtd_tlsd
column.description: Month-to-date total lines sold metric for the reporting context. It summarizes line-selling activity across the month where populated.

column.name: mtd_ulsd
column.description: Month-to-date unique lines sold metric for the reporting context. This helps evaluate product assortment breadth over the month.

column.name: mtd_eco
column.description: Month-to-date effective coverage outlet count or indicator total. Analysts can use it for cumulative coverage tracking against outlet universe or focus targets.

column.name: mtd_p3_nsv
column.description: Month-to-date P3 net sales value. Use it for cumulative P3 sales performance and target achievement calculations.

column.name: mtd_p2_nsv
column.description: Month-to-date P2 net sales value. Use it for cumulative P2 sales performance and target achievement calculations.

column.name: universe
column.description: Count of eligible outlets in the employee, beat, territory, or reporting universe. It is used as the denominator for coverage and productivity measures when populated.

column.name: lppc
column.description: Lines per productive call, a productivity metric showing average line breadth per productive call. Higher values generally indicate stronger cross-selling or assortment depth.

column.name: productivity_pct
column.description: Productivity percentage, typically productive calls divided by total calls. Analysts should interpret it as the effectiveness of visits in generating productive outcomes.

column.name: pc_target
column.description: Target number of productive calls for the reporting period or employee context. Use it as the denominator for productive call achievement calculations.

column.name: lppc_target
column.description: Target for lines per productive call. It provides the benchmark for evaluating selling breadth productivity.

column.name: p3_target_nsv
column.description: Target net sales value for the P3 bucket. Analysts should compare mtd_p3_nsv or aggregated p3_nsv against this value for achievement reporting.

column.name: p2_target_nsv
column.description: Target net sales value for the P2 bucket. Use it to assess P2 sales achievement against plan.

column.name: focus_target_vol
column.description: Volume target for focus products or focus categories. This is used to measure execution against specific focus-product campaigns.

column.name: focus_target_eco
column.description: Effective coverage target for focus products or focus outlets. Analysts can compare focus ECO actuals against this target to evaluate execution reach.

column.name: pc_achievement_pct
column.description: Percentage achievement of productive call target. It is generally calculated as actual productive calls divided by pc_target.

column.name: p3_achievement_pct
column.description: Percentage achievement of P3 NSV target. It indicates how much of the planned P3 net sales has been delivered.

column.name: p2_achievement_pct
column.description: Percentage achievement of P2 NSV target. It indicates progress against the planned P2 net sales objective.

column.name: new_outlet_count
column.description: Count of outlets classified as newly added or newly activated in the reporting context. Use it for outlet expansion and acquisition tracking.

column.name: new_outlet_p3_nsv
column.description: P3 net sales value generated from new outlets. This helps analysts evaluate sales contribution from newly acquired outlets.
